title: dicliptera cicatricosa
intro: Dicliptera cicatricosa, also known as Mexican false sage, is a perennial herb native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its fragrant, tubular flowers and distinctive leaf scars. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Its health benefits include treating respiratory issues, wounds, and skin infections, as well as reducing fever and relieving headaches. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the Dicliptera cicatricosa plant can be made into a tea or infused oil to harness its medicinal properties.
